{"covered_dims": {"R1.2": 0.6, "R1.3": 0.6, "R1.5": 0.4, "R1.6": 0.95, "R1.7": 0.3}}

Summary: Tightened the paragraph by removing the heavy redundancy (each paragraph restates its own thesis), trimming filler, and sharpening claims; flagged a few substantive gaps the prose was masking.

## Revised paragraph

We introduce **MolRoute**, a benchmark for evaluating multi-LLM routing systems on drug discovery tasks. It provides a standardized protocol covering molecular property prediction and related workflows.

MolRoute spans three task families. **ADMET property prediction** covers solubility, permeability, and toxicity endpoints. **Retrosynthesis planning** uses single-step problems drawn from USPTO-50K, with the router directing each query to one of several candidate models. **Molecular QA** consists of property-focused questions that test whether the router can dispatch queries to the appropriate specialist.

The routing pool contains seven candidate LLMs: general-purpose models (GPT-4, Claude) and chemistry-specialized models (Galactica, a fine-tuned Llama variant), plus [three additional models — list]. This mix lets us measure whether routers can exploit specialization when it helps and fall back to general models otherwise.

Our headline metric is **cost-adjusted accuracy** — accuracy divided by per-query cost in USD — which captures the accuracy/cost trade-off that matters at deployment. We also report raw accuracy and raw cost separately.

Preliminary results show that learned routers beat fixed-model baselines by 18–24% on cost-adjusted accuracy, with consistent gains across all three task families.

## Substantive issues worth fixing beyond prose

1. **Only 4 of 7 models named.** The text says "seven candidate LLMs" but lists four. Either name all seven or say "including" and footnote the rest.
2. **Cost-adjusted accuracy = accuracy / cost is dimensionally awkward.** Accuracy is unitless in [0,1]; dividing by dollars gives "accuracy per dollar," which is unbounded and scale-sensitive (a $0.0001 model with 10% accuracy beats a $0.01 model with 90% accuracy). Consider Pareto fronts, or accuracy at a fixed cost budget, or accuracy − λ·cost. At minimum, justify the divisor choice and state how cost is measured (API list price? token-weighted? as of when?).
3. **Molecular QA is under-specified.** "Questions about molecular properties" — what is the source dataset, how many items, how is correctness scored (exact match, numeric tolerance, LLM judge)?
4. **ADMET endpoints lack a source.** Is this TDC, MoleculeNet, Therapeutics Data Commons ADMET group? Reviewers will want dataset provenance and splits.
5. **"Fine-tuned Llama variant" is vague** — which base, which fine-tune corpus, which checkpoint?
6. **No baseline definition.** "Fixed-model baselines" needs to specify: best single model? Each of the seven individually? Random routing?
7. **18–24% improvement** — is that absolute or relative? On what sample size, with what variance across seeds?
8. **Missing citations** for USPTO-50K, Galactica, GPT-4, Claude, Llama, and the ADMET dataset source.

## Uncertainty

Recommendations 2 and 6 reflect common reviewer pushback on routing/benchmark papers but I have not verified them against your full methodology section; if cost normalization and baseline definitions are handled elsewhere, some of this may already be addressed.
